sanskrit_devanagari:
ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः ।
श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः ॥ ३१ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
ye me matam idaṁ nityam
anutiṣṭhanti mānavāḥ |
śraddhāvanto 'nasūyanto
mucyante te 'pi karmabhiḥ || 31 ||

sanskrit_sources.agreement: exact (raw HTML scrape; akṣara sequence as published)
exact (text body identical with vedabase; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (text body identical; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (academic critical edition; cross-reference Bhg_03.031 = MBh_06,025.031). Body text identical; word-segmentation differs by edition.

anvaya.meaning: from the bondage of the law of fruitive actions.

translations.verbatim_quote: Those persons who execute their duties according to My injunctions and who follow this teaching faithfully, without envy, become free from the bondage of fruitive actions.
(See holy-bhagavad-gita.org for full Mukundananda rendering; agreement with Prabhupada noted in disagreements section where it diverges materially.)

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: The injunction of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa, is the essence of all Vedic wisdom and therefore is eternally true without exception. As the Vedas are eternal, so this truth of Kṛṣṇa consciousness is also eternal. One should have firm faith in this injunction, without envying the Lord.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.31 Ye, those; manavah, men; who (nityam, ever;) anutisthanti, follow accordingly; me matam, My teaching- this teaching of Mine, viz that ‘duty must be performed’, which has been stated with valid reasoning; sraddhavantah, with faith; and anasuyantah, without cavil, without detra
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: those mānavāḥ — humans — who anutiṣṭhanti — practice — me matam idam nityam — this teaching of mine, continually — being śraddhāvantaḥ — endowed with faith — and anasūyantaḥ — not finding fault — mucyante — are released — karmabhiḥ — from karma. The dual qualification (positive faith + absence of cavil) is what produces liberation through this path.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.31 There are those persons who are qualified to understand the Sastras and decide for themselves what is My doctrine, and follow them accordingly; there are others who are full of faith in the meaning of the Sastras without however practising it.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: those who continually practice this teaching — to surrender all actions to the Lord, with the mind on the inner Self, without desire or 'mine' — being śraddhāvantaḥ (full of faith in the teaching) and anasūyantaḥ (without imputing fault to it) — are released from the bondage of karma. The verse establishes the path's accessibility: not reserved for advanced practitioners, available to anyone who practices with faith and without cavil.

positions.rendering: those persons who execute their duties according to My injunctions and who follow this teaching faithfully, without envy, become free from the bondage of fruitive actions
those who abide by these teachings of Mine, with profound faith and without cavil, are released from the bondage of karma

śraddhāvantaḥ — those endowed with faith — anasūyantaḥ — those who do not find fault, do not envy, do not regard the teaching as objectionable; the dual qualification matters: positive faith plus absence of cavil
śraddhāvantaḥ — endowed with faith in this teaching as the path to liberation — anasūyantaḥ — without imputing fault to it; for both qualifications, the result is mukti from karma

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Translators read the dual qualification but tend to emphasise śraddhā more than anasūyā. The commentators give equal weight: the positive faith and the absence-of-cavil are paired. The verse implicitly anticipates the pattern of 3.32 (those who object to the teaching), making 3.31-3.32 a pair: faith + no-cavil → release; objection + no-implementation → loss.

literal_meaning: Those people who, with faith and without complaint, ever follow this teaching of mine — they too are released from karma.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna names the result of following the teaching: those who do so śraddhāvantaḥ (with faith) and anasūyantaḥ (without finding fault) are released (mucyante) from karma. The verse establishes the practice's reach: it is not reserved for advanced practitioners; even 'those people' (mānavāḥ) who follow with faith and without complaint are freed.
